Supermodel Cara Delevigne was spotted in a rather strange ensemble leaving her weekend Glastonbury.
The model of the moment was papped wearing a brightly patterned tracksuit topped off with a cat mask.
This followed her posting a photo wearing a gorilla mask on Instagram.
But Cara isn't the only Glastonbury guest who chose a mask to accessorise with!
Here are the best offerings from the weekend.
World music act Goat were all sporting masks when they took to the West Holts Stage on Friday.
Their combination of psychadelic sounds and costumes were a huge hit with the crowd.
Bombay Royale sported classic black masks for their performance.
Very Zorro, very cool.
Cara's sister Poppy was spotted wearing this Native American style headdress with her aviators and denim shorts.
We love the co-ordinating boots too!
Meet Mexican thumb wrestling team Luscious Libras.
They made their masks and costumes themselves and toured the site exhibiting power lunges, shiny capes and amazing thumb wrestling skills.
Want to sport a mask at a summer festival?
Why not try our Pure Neon Acid Lace Mask for £.?
Or how about our Pure Neon Yellow Lace also £.?
Perfect for the UV lights in a dance tent!
The author of this post is Emma Riordan, Samantha Peach's blog and social media intern.

A South American courtyard party!
Farewell summer and Welcome autumn.
Featuring Los Galanes with their irresistible swinging sound.
Los Galanes is an eight piece Latin ensemble made up of a vibrant international group of professional musicians with a passion to play and entertain Latin and French rhythms to one and all.
Los Galanes boasts an eclectic mix of members hailing from all over the world.
Mambo - Salsa - Bosa Nova - Bolero - Cha-Cha - Tango - Merengue -Jazz/Latin and much more!
Fiesta Beat!
Calling us in to start our celebration will be the hot beats of the Sambassadors.
Fiesta Play!
will commence with Capoeira, a Brazilian martial art, which features kicks, takedowns, inversions,balance, acrobatics, flow, self expression, culture, traditions and history all blended together through music and songs.
These rhythms dictate whether the game is played as a fight or a dance.
Instructor Cenoura, recently returned from Brazil and his students will show us how!
Fiesta Dance!
will continue with a Colombian Dance performance followed by the teaching of some dance moves that will allow us to really get into the groove of LOS GALANES.
Fiesta Food!
Traditional Colombian food will be for sale throughout the evening.
There will be delightful places set to sit and eat.
Fiesta Bar!
will be operating with an added Latin Flavour!
